visa_free.notes: US citizens are fully exempt from both visa and eTA requirements for all methods of travel. Must carry valid US passport or other accepted travel document (birth certificate, certificate of citizenship/naturalization, certificate of Indian status + photo ID, or US enhanced driver's license). May stay normally up to 6 months.

eta.notes: Visa-exempt nationals require eTA for air travel only. No eTA needed when arriving by land or sea (except from Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon by sea). eTA costs CAD $7, valid up to 5 years or until passport expires.

British citizens require eTA for air travel only. Also covers: British nationals overseas; British overseas citizens re-admissible to the United Kingdom; British subjects with a right of abode in the United Kingdom.
British overseas territory citizens with citizenship through birth, descent, naturalization or registration in Anguilla require eTA for air travel only.
British overseas territory citizens with citizenship through birth, descent, naturalization or registration in Bermuda require eTA for air travel only.

eta.nationality: Taiwan (ordinary passport with personal identification number)
eta.notes: Must hold an ordinary passport issued by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Taiwan that includes the personal identification number. Holders of a Taiwan passport without a personal identification number require a visitor visa.

visa_policy.notes: Canada uses two entry mechanisms: (1) Visitor Visa (Temporary Resident Visa) required for most nationals; (2) eTA (Electronic Travel Authorization, CAD $7, valid 5 years or until passport expires) required for visa-exempt nationals travelling by air. US citizens and US Lawful Permanent Residents are fully exempt from both. French citizens residing in and travelling directly from Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon are also exempt from both. Visa-exempt nationals arriving by land or sea do NOT need an eTA (exception: arriving by sea from Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon requires an eTA). Max stay is normally up to 6 months per visit. As of May 26, 2026, Indonesia and Malaysia added to the conditional eTA eligibility list (eta-x). Romania eTA applies to electronic passport holders only. Taiwan eTA applies only to passports that include a personal identification number. Israel eTA applies only to holders of national Israeli passports (not Travel Document in Lieu of National Passport). Papua New Guinea, Qatar, Samoa, Solomon Islands, and Vatican City State confirmed on eTA list as of June 2026 official page.

credential.subtype: valid permanent resident (green) card (Form I-551) or equivalent valid proof of US lawful permanent resident status

conditional_access.conditions: As of April 26, 2022, lawful permanent residents of the United States are exempt from both visa and eTA requirements for all methods of travel to Canada (air, land, sea). By air: must carry valid passport from country of nationality (or equivalent acceptable travel document) AND valid green card or equivalent proof of US LPR status. By land or water directly from the US or Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon: only the valid green card or equivalent proof of status is required (no passport needed). Acceptable status documents include: valid Form I-551, foreign passport with valid temporary I-551 stamp (ADIT), foreign passport with temporary I-551 printed notation on immigrant visa with CBP admission stamp, expired Form I-551 with Form I-797 for pending I-751/I-829/I-90, valid re-entry permit (Form I-327), Form I-94 with unexpired ADIT stamp and passport-style photo.

credential.subtype: valid US nonimmigrant visa (must be valid on day of eTA application; not required to be valid on day of travel to Canada)

conditional_access.conditions: Citizens of the 17 eligible visa-required countries may apply for a Canadian eTA (instead of a visitor visa) if they currently hold a valid US nonimmigrant visa. The US nonimmigrant visa must be valid on the day of applying for the eTA, but does not need to be valid on the day of travel to Canada. Travel must be by air only (flying to or transiting through a Canadian airport). By land, bus, train or boat, a visitor visa is still required. Temporary stay only (normally up to 6 months). Traveller should bring old passport containing the US visa when flying. Effective May 26, 2026: Indonesia (IDN) and Malaysia (MYS) added to the eligible country list.

credential.subtype: Canadian visitor visa (temporary resident visa) held in the past 10 years (even if expired)

conditional_access.conditions: Citizens of the 17 eligible visa-required countries may apply for a Canadian eTA (instead of a new visitor visa) if they have held a Canadian visitor visa (temporary resident visa) at any time in the past 10 years (even if that visa has since expired). Travel must be by air only (flying to or transiting through a Canadian airport). By land, bus, train or boat, a visitor visa is still required. Temporary stay only (normally up to 6 months). If the traveller already holds a currently valid Canadian visitor visa, they should continue to use it — no eTA needed. Effective May 26, 2026: Indonesia (IDN) and Malaysia (MYS) added to the eligible country list.

Transit Without Visa (TWOV) Program: Citizens of Indonesia, Philippines, Thailand, and Taiwan (holding a passport without a personal identification number) may transit through Canada without a Canadian visa if they hold a valid US visa and are travelling to/from the United States. Requirements: (1) hold a valid US visa; (2) have a confirmed airline ticket for a flight to/from the US that leaves Canada within 24 hours of arrival; (3) travel with a participating airline (Air Canada, Air Canada Rouge, Air China, Cathay Pacific, China Airlines, China Eastern, China Southern, Hainan Airlines, Jazz Air, Philippines Airlines, WestJet, Xiamen Airlines); (4) transit through specific Canadian airports only (Toronto Pearson Terminal 1 only, Vancouver, Calgary, or Winnipeg); (5) must remain in the international sterile transit area and not leave the airport; (6) depart Canada within 24 hours. For travel FROM the US through Canada, traveller must also meet US immigration conditions (entered US with valid visa, no removal/deportation order). This is a transit-only exemption — no entry into Canada permitted. Note: Indonesia nationals who do not meet TWOV requirements must apply for a transit visa (unlike PHL/THA/TWN who in some cases may apply for an eTA).

cbi.notes: Canada does not offer a Citizenship-by-Investment program. There is no direct pathway to citizenship through financial investment alone. All investment-related immigration pathways lead first to permanent residence, and citizenship requires meeting physical presence requirements (1,095 days in any 5-year period as a permanent resident). Confirmed by absence on official IRCC Canada.ca pages.

programs.name: Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) – Entrepreneur/Business Streams
programs.notes: As of 2026, investment-based immigration to Canada runs exclusively through Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) entrepreneur streams after the federal Start-up Visa Program was paused. Investment requirements vary by province; minimum starts at CAD $100,000 for some rural streams and CAD $200,000 for metropolitan streams according to non-official sources — province-specific official amounts must be verified on individual provincial government websites. Nominees then apply for federal PR through IRCC. Citizenship requires 1,095 days physical presence as a PR in any 5-year period.

programs.name: Federal Self-Employed Persons Program
programs.notes: For self-employed persons with relevant experience in cultural activities or athletics (world-class level) or farm management. Must have at least 2 years relevant self-employment experience in the 5 years before applying. No minimum investment amount — program is experience-based. Leads to permanent residence and eventually citizenship.

programs.name: Express Entry – Federal Skilled Worker Program
programs.eligibility: Skilled workers with at least 1 year continuous paid full-time work experience (or equivalent part-time: 1,560 hours) in a NOC TEER 0, 1, 2, or 3 occupation; language proficiency; education; minimum 67 CRS points
programs.processing_time: 6 months (service standard for 80% of complete applications)
programs.notes: Canada's flagship points-based skilled worker permanent residence pathway. Candidates create an online profile, enter the pool, and receive Invitations to Apply (ITA) through rounds of invitations. Category-based selection rounds for 2026 prioritize healthcare/social services, trades, and STEM occupations.

programs.name: Express Entry – Canadian Experience Class
programs.eligibility: At least 1 year of skilled work experience in Canada in NOC TEER 0, 1, 2, or 3; language proficiency
programs.processing_time: 6 months (service standard for 80% of complete applications)
programs.notes: For those with recent Canadian work experience. Part of the Express Entry system.

programs.name: Express Entry – Federal Skilled Trades Program
programs.eligibility: Skilled trade workers with qualifying job offer or certificate of qualification; at least 2 years paid full-time work experience in a skilled trade within 5 years before applying; language proficiency
programs.processing_time: 6 months (service standard for 80% of complete applications)
programs.notes: For tradespeople such as carpenters, plumbers, electricians. Part of the Express Entry system.

programs.name: Global Skills Strategy (GSS) – 2-Week Work Permit Processing
programs.eligibility: Workers in NOC TEER 0 (management) or TEER 1 (university degree required) occupations; employer must qualify; biometrics submitted within 2 weeks of instruction letter
programs.processing_time: 2 weeks (goal for 80% of eligible complete applications)
programs.notes: Fast-track temporary work permit processing for highly skilled workers. Canada aims to process 80% of eligible complete GSS work permit applications within 2 weeks. Applies to both LMIA-required and LMIA-exempt positions at TEER 0 and TEER 1 levels.

programs.name: International Experience Canada (IEC) – Working Holiday
programs.eligibility: Youth aged 18-35 (18-30 in some countries) from 35+ partner countries; must hold valid passport from eligible country; meet health and character requirements
programs.notes: Working holiday open work permit allowing youth to work for most employers anywhere in Canada. Also includes Young Professionals and International Co-op/Internship categories. Pools for 2026 season are open. Participating countries include Australia, France, Germany, UK, Japan, Korea, Ireland and many others.

validation_notes: ADDED to eta list: Papua New Guinea (PNG) — confirmed on official entry-requirements-country.html page (last updated 2026-06-05).
ADDED to eta list: Qatar (QAT) — confirmed on official entry-requirements-country.html page.
ADDED to eta list: Samoa (WSM) — confirmed on official entry-requirements-country.html page.
ADDED to eta list: Solomon Islands (SLB) — confirmed on official entry-requirements-country.html page.
ADDED to eta list: Vatican City State (VAT) — confirmed on official entry-requirements-country.html page; must have passport or travel document issued by the Vatican.

EXPANDED United Kingdom entry: Prior record had a single 'United Kingdom' entry and some British Overseas Territory entries. Official page distinguishes: British citizen; British national overseas; British overseas citizen (re-admissible to UK); British overseas territory citizens (with specific territories listed: Anguilla, Bermuda, British Virgin Islands, Cayman Islands, Falkland Islands/Malvinas, Gibraltar, Montserrat, Pitcairn Island, Saint Helena, Turks and Caicos Islands); British subject with right of abode in UK. Added missing territories: British Virgin Islands, Cayman Islands, Falkland Islands (Malvinas), Gibraltar, Montserrat, Pitcairn Island, Saint Helena.

CORRECTED Romania: Prior record had Romania as a plain eTA entry. Official page specifies eTA applies to electronic passport holders only; holders of non-electronic (temporary) Romanian passports require a visitor visa. Entry now includes this caveat.

CORRECTED Israel: Prior record noted 'Israeli passport holders only' vaguely. Official page specifies 'must have a national Israeli passport'; holders of an Israeli Travel Document in Lieu of National Passport are listed as visa-required. Note updated accordingly.

CORRECTED Taiwan: Prior record noted eTA for 'ordinary passports issued by Taiwan' without clarifying the personal ID number requirement. Official page specifies eTA requires ordinary passport that INCLUDES personal identification number; those without the PIN are visa-required. Note updated accordingly.
CORRECTED France note: Updated to reflect official language that French citizens living in and travelling directly from Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon are fully exempt (no visa, no eTA).

UPDATED conditional_access: Indonesia and Malaysia confirmed as added to the eta-x eligible country list effective May 26, 2026, per official eta-x.html page (modified 2026-05-26). The eligible_nationalities arrays in both eta-x credential entries already included IDN and MYS and are confirmed correct.

UPDATED TWOV: Indonesia note updated to reflect that Indonesia nationals who do not meet TWOV requirements must apply for a transit visa (different from PHL/THA/TWN who may be eligible for eTA in some cases), per the updated TWOV eligibility page.

REMOVED from eta list: Prior separate entry for 'Anguilla (British Overseas Territory passport holders)' and 'Bermuda (British Overseas Territory passport holders)' and 'Turks and Caicos Islands (British Overseas Territory passport holders)' replaced with full expanded British breakdown covering all territories.
gaps: The complete list of visa-required countries not eligible for conditional eTA could not be exhaustively verified entry-by-entry due to page length; the full list was captured from the official entry-requirements-country.html page.

Provincial Nominee Program entrepreneur stream minimum investment amounts vary by province; exact official figures for each province not included here — province-specific official websites must be consulted.
Canada does not have a dedicated digital nomad visa; digital nomads work under visitor status up to 6 months without a formal separate visa category.
No freedom-of-movement agreements applicable to Canada as a member — Canada is not part of Schengen, CARICOM freedom of movement, or any formal common travel area.

Diplomatic/service passport bilateral visa waiver agreements: No country-specific bilateral diplomatic passport visa waiver list was findable from official Canadian sources. IRCC operational bulletins manual page was not fetched in this pass.
India OCI cardholder status: No official Canadian government source grants special entry privileges to India OCI cardholders. Canada treats OCI holders by their passport nationality.

China Transit Program (separate from TWOV): A separate China Transit Program exists for Chinese passport holders connecting through Canada. Not enumerated here as it has different eligibility criteria. Official URL: https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/corporate/publications-manuals/operational-bulletins-manuals/temporary-residents/visitors/china-transit-program.html
Start-up Visa Program status: Paused as of January 1, 2026 per IRCC. Official page was not re-scraped in this pass to verify any resumption.

outbound_official.note: No official Canada government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The Government of Canada does not publish a consolidated list of countries where Canadian ordinary-passport holders may travel without a prior consular visa. Instead, travel.gc.ca uses a per-destination model: each country advisory at travel.gc.ca/destinations/{country} contains an 'Entry and exit requirements' section that states whether Canadians need a visa. The nearest thematic page is travel.gc.ca/travelling/documents/visas (General visa information for Canadians travelling abroad), which explains the concept of visas/eTAs but does not enumerate destinations. Global Affairs Canada (international.gc.ca) similarly does not publish an outbound visa-free matrix for Canadian passport holders. Source searched: travel.gc.ca, canada.ca, international.gc.ca — confirmed no consolidated outbound list exists as of 2026-06-14.

sources_checked.note: Successfully scraped via Firecrawl stealth proxy on 2026-06-14. Page last modified 2026-06-05. Confirmed US LPR exemption (as of April 26, 2022), eTA-required countries list reference, and conditional eTA for select visa-required countries.

sources_checked.description: Official IRCC eTA eligibility for visa-required country nationals (eta-x)

Confirmed 17 eligible countries including Indonesia and Malaysia as newly added (effective May 26, 2026), exact 3-part eligibility criteria (US nonimmigrant visa OR Canadian visitor visa in past 10 years), and air-travel-only restriction.

sources_checked.description: Official IRCC entry requirements by country – full visa/eTA/exemption listings
sources_checked.note: Successfully scraped via Firecrawl stealth proxy on 2026-06-14. Page last modified 2026-06-05. Confirmed complete eTA country list (including Papua New Guinea, Qatar, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Vatican City State, and full breakdown of British categories), Romania electronic-passport-only caveat, Israel national-passport-only caveat, Taiwan personal-ID-number caveat, US LPR rules by travel mode, and full no-visa/no-eTA exemption list.

sources_checked.description: Official IRCC Transit Without Visa (TWOV) Program eligibility page
sources_checked.note: Successfully scraped via Firecrawl stealth proxy on 2026-06-14. Page last modified 2023-09-28. Confirmed TWOV applies to Indonesia, Philippines, Thailand, and Taiwan passport holders holding valid US visa, transiting to/from the US, within 24 hours, at specific airports, with participating airlines. Note: Indonesia nationals who fail TWOV requirements must apply for transit visa (different treatment from PHL/THA/TWN).

visa_types.name: Temporary Resident Visa (Visitor Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Tourism, visiting family, short business trips
visa_types.notes: Apply online via IRCC. Most nationalities require a TRV; some require only an eTA. Fee is CAD 100.

visa_types.name: Study Permit
visa_types.purpose: Studying at a Designated Learning Institution (DLI) for programs longer than 6 months
visa_types.notes: Requires acceptance letter from a DLI. Fee is CAD 150. Students can work up to 20 hours/week off-campus.

visa_types.name: Work Permit (Employer-Specific)
visa_types.purpose: Working for a specific Canadian employer
visa_types.notes: Most require a Labour Market Impact Assessment (LMIA) from the employer. CUSMA/USMCA and CETA provide streamlined pathways for US, Mexican, and EU nationals. Fee is CAD 155.

visa_types.name: International Experience Canada (IEC) – Working Holiday
visa_types.purpose: Living and working in Canada for up to 1–2 years (youth, select nationalities)
visa_types.notes: Available for youth 18–35 from over 30 countries via bilateral agreements. Apply through IEC portal. Fee is CAD 256 (includes open work permit holder fee).
